Cadbury second largest confectionery company world.
2014, Cadbury sold over million chocolate products Zealand, average they sell million bars every year worldwide.
However, there dark side this company produces confectionery beloved many.
million children between ages working developing countries, least million whom work full time.
Thousands child slaves work cocoa industry.
They denied right education, made work dangerous conditions abused their employers.
These children often unpaid, next nothing.
This where Fairtrade comes
Fairtrade movement helps make cocoa farming places like Ivory Coast more sustainable guaranteeing farmers minimum prices their cocoa, providing Fairtrade premiums invest local communities.
This helps farmers provide better futures themselves their families.
Fairtrade certification label products help consumers ensure that food they buying produced ethically.
Cadbury website states that Cadbury been supporting Fairtrade since 2010.
However, only their Dairy Milk chocolate Fair Trade certified.
This means that their other products contain cocoa that produced using slave labour.
calling boycott Cadbury products that Fair Trade certified, hopes encouraging Cadbury switch ethically sourced cocoa.
human rights thousands children stake, need take action NOW.
Lisa Davy needs your help with "Cadbury: Stop Exploitation African Children: Boycott Non-Fairtrade Cadbury Products".
Join Lisa supporters today.
